channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Élevé] Australian energy provider Origin says data breach exposes client data
item.description: Origin Energy has confirmed that an unauthorized party accessed and subsequently leaked customer data online, exposing sensitive personally identifiable information (PII), among others. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Fake Claude app promoted by Bing ads pushes SectopRAT malware
item.description: A malvertising campaign on the Bing search service is pushing a fake Claude desktop app installer hosted on a legitimate Claude.ai domain to deliver the SectopRAT malware. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
item.description:
A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client.
The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organization's entire email…

item.title: [Faible] International alert spotlights Russia-linked attacks on Zimbra webmail
item.description: A Kremlin-backed group known as Laundry Bear has been using a zero-click phishing technique to break into Zimbra webmail accounts worldwide, the U.S. and other nations said.

item.title: [Moyen] Russian hackers exploit Zimbra zero-click flaw for email theft
item.description: CISA is warning that the Russian state-sponsored hacking group Laundry Bear, also known as Void Blizzard, is targeting organizations using Zimbra Collaboration email servers by combining phishing attacks with the…

item.title: [Faible] Hackers abuse Notepad++ plugins to stealthily install malware
item.description: Ukraine's CERT has uncovered attacks distributing an archive containing the legitimate Notepad++ application and a malicious utility called LunchPoke disguised as a plugin to establish persistence. [...]

item.title: [Faible] State Department imposes visa restrictions on foreign cyber scammers
item.description: Individuals connected to transnational cyber-scam operations face U.S. visa restrictions under a new policy announced by Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft 365 outage affects Teams, SharePoint and other services
item.description: Microsoft Teams and several Microsoft 365 services are experiencing an ongoing outage, with users reporting problems accessing Teams, SharePoint, Excel and the Microsoft 365 Admin Center. [...]

item.title: [Faible] ThreatsDay: Android Spyware, PLC Attacks, AI Image Prompt Injection + 12 More Stories
item.description:
Most of this week's trouble came dressed as something useful.
A package stole data. A fake extension opened remote access. A safety app became spyware. An image gave hidden orders to an AI agent. Other threats hid in…

item.title: [Critique] Cyberattaque à Drancy : tous les serveurs débranchés, la mairie refuse la rançon et redoute une fuite de données
item.description: Depuis le 3 juillet 2026, la mairie de Drancy fait face à une importante attaque par rançongiciel. Les... L’article Cyberattaque à Drancy : tous les serveurs débranchés, la mairie refuse la rançon et redoute une fuite…

item.title: [Faible] FedRAMP Rev5 Is Ending: What the 20x Transition Really Requires
item.description: FedRAMP 20X replaces point-in-time assessments with continuous, machine-readable evidence that demonstrates security controls are working. Anecdotes explains what the transition from Rev5 to FedRAMP 20X means and how…

item.title: [Faible] Immobilière Tariot : fuite des plans de logements, RIB et dossiers de copropriétaires marseillais
item.description: Immobilière Tariot, agence immobilière et syndic de copropriétés basée à Marseille, fait l’objet d’une revendication publiée le 22... L’article Immobilière Tariot : fuite des plans de logements, RIB et dossiers de…

item.title: [Faible] Aide à Dom 74 : 92 candidatures en fuite, les coordonnées de demandeurs d’emploi exposées
item.description: Le hacker misere revendique l’extraction de données depuis le site aideadom74.fr, spécialisé dans les services d’aide à domicile... L’article Aide à Dom 74 : 92 candidatures en fuite, les coordonnées de demandeurs…

item.title: [Moyen] Claude Cowork Flaw Could Let AI Agent Escape Its VM and Access Mac Files
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a sandbox escape vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Cowork that makes it possible to break out of the confines of a Linux virtual machine (VM) within which the agent runs to…

item.title: [Élevé] Major Australian energy supplier confirms customer data compromised
item.description: Origin Energy said it was working to figure out how many Australians were affected by a recent data breach.

item.title: [Critique] Chaos Ransomware Uses msaRAT to Route C2 Traffic Through Headless Chrome and Edge
item.description: The Chaos ransomware group ran its command-and-control through the victim's own browser. Cisco Talos on Thursday detailed msaRAT, the Rust implant behind it, found on a compromised Windows machine ahead of the…

item.title: [Faible] EU fines Google $1 billion for search, app store antitrust violations
item.description: The European Commission fined Google €890 million ($1 billion) on Thursday after finding the company had violated the European Union's Digital Markets Act (DMA), which ensures fair online competition. [...]

item.title: [Faible] China-Nexus JadeProx Uses New TriBack Loader in Government and Healthcare Attacks
item.description: An exposed Alibaba Cloud server has revealed a China-nexus operation that Group-IB tracks as JadeProx. The cluster has targeted government, healthcare, and education organizations across Asia and Latin America with a…

item.title: [Faible] UK and partners expose Russian state-supported actors for new ‘zero-click’ phishing campaign targeting Western organisations
item.description: GCHQ’s National Cyber Security Centre and international partners issue warning as ‘LAUNDRY BEAR’ cyber threat group exposed for targeted phishing campaign

item.title: [Faible] How Synthetic Identity Fraud is Coming for Machine Identities
item.description: Most people understand identity theft as an attacker stealing a real person's sensitive information and impersonating them. Synthetic identity fraud is much harder to catch. Instead of stealing a real identity, the…

item.title: [Moyen] New RefluXFS Linux flaw lets attackers gain root privileges
item.description: A nine-year-old race condition vulnerability in the Linux kernel's XFS filesystem, tracked as CVE-2026-64600, allows local attackers to overwrite protected files and gain root privileges. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Attackers Weaponize GitHub Actions Runners to Target cPanel and WHM Servers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a large-scale campaign that has turned compromised GitHub repositories into distributed attack infrastructure designed to target cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM)…

item.title: [Critique] New msaRAT malware uses Chrome, Edge browsers to route C2 traffic
item.description: The Chaos ransomware gang is using a new backdoor dubbed msaRAT that hides command-and-control (C2) communication by routing it through the Chrome or Edge browsers. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Google Adds Selfie Video Recovery for Users Locked Out of Their Accounts
item.description:
Google on Thursday announced a new way for users to sign-in to their accounts by letting them take a selfie video.
The selfie for sign-in, per the tech giant, is another option on top of existing recovery methods to…

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft working to fix Exchange Online mailbox quarantine issue
item.description: Microsoft is working to resolve an ongoing Exchange Online issue that has been mistakenly quarantining customers' mailboxes since Sunday. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Check Point warns of SmartConsole zero-day exploited in attacks
item.description: Israeli cybersecurity firm Check Point Software has addressed an actively exploited zero-day flaw in the company's SmartConsole graphical user interface (GUI) admin panel. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs
item.description:
RefluXFS, a new Linux kernel flaw disclosed on July 22 and tracked as CVE-2026-64600, lets an unprivileged local user overwrite root-owned files on an XFS filesystem and gain persistent root access.
Qualys said default…

item.title: [Moyen] Check Point Patches Exploited SmartConsole Flaw Allowing Full Admin Access
item.description: Check Point has released security updates to address multiple vulnerabilities impacting Security Management and Multi-Domain Management (MDSM) products, including a critical flaw that has come under active exploitation…

item.title: [Critique] Swiss train maker Stadler refuses Everest $12 million ransomware demand
item.description: Stadler Rail said it will not make a $12.3 million ransom payment after cybercriminals stole technical data from a supplier's file-sharing platform.

item.title: [Faible] Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases
item.description: The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Extension of CISA 2015 info-sharing protections passes as part of House’s defense bill
item.description: A 10-year renewal of the cybersecurity information-sharing law known as CISA 2015 passed as part of the House's fiscal 2027 defense authorization bill.
